requirement_blocks.rules_and_restrictions: Note 1: Students must take CHEM 130 and CHEM 125/126 OR CHEM 210 and CHEM 211. If you have a satisfactory score or grade in Chemistry AP, A-Level, IB Exams, or transfer credit from another institution for CHEM 130/125/126, you will have met the Chemistry Core Requirement. Note 2: If you have a satisfactory score or grade in Physics AP, A-Level, IB Exams, or transfer credit from another institution for PHYSICS 140/141 and 240/241, you will have met the Physics Core.
Includes ECON 101 or 102. Other courses not specified in this document.
Civil Engineering students must earn a C- or better in all courses whose categories are marked with a plus.

Civil Engineering students must earn a C- or better in all courses whose categories are marked with a plus. Students must take CEE 230 OR CEE 375. Note 3: CEE may accept equivalent courses offered by other departments in the College of Engineering, with permission from the program advisor. Note 8: CEE 402 must be taken in the last Winter semester.
Civil Engineering students must earn a C- or better in all courses whose categories are marked with a plus.

Civil Engineering students must earn a C- or better in all courses whose categories are marked with a plus. Note 4: At least five of the seven program electives are required. Note 5: A sixth program elective may count as a technical elective.

Civil Engineering students must earn a C- or better in all courses whose categories are marked with a plus. Note 5: A sixth program elective may count as a technical elective. Note 6: At least one of the three technical electives must be a design course (design courses are marked with an *). Note 7: CEE may accept study abroad courses as a technical elective with prior approval of the department.
